description:
You are the backup administrator for a small development host.  
Your job is to generate a **package-style archive** of all current project
material that lives in:

        /home/user/projects

and to leave behind a machine-readable manifest that a nightly robot can
consume later.

The work must be finished by “2024-06-30”, so every artefact you create
must embed the string “20240630” exactly once in its file-name.

STEP-BY-STEP GOALS
1. Create the output directory “/home/user/archives” if it is missing.  
   • The directory must end up with permissions 0755 (drwxr-xr-x).

2. Produce a manifest file named
        /home/user/archives/projects_manifest_20240630.log
   The manifest has to list **every regular file** below
   “/home/user/projects”, sorted alphabetically by its *archive-relative*
   path (see the Format section).  
   Do **NOT** include directories, symlinks or the manifest itself.  
   The robot relies on the exact byte counts shown in the example below.

3. Build a compressed tarball named
        /home/user/archives/projects_backup_20240630.tar.gz
   Requirements for the archive:
   • Compression: gzip, maximum compression (equivalent to “‐9”).  
   • Layout inside the archive: paths must begin with “projects/…”.
     (Hint: run tar from “/home/user” so the directory prefix is kept.)  
   • The manifest file created in step 2 must **NOT** be inside the tarball.

4. When everything is done, write the following three lines **exactly** to
   standard output so that the CI runner can capture them (no extra spaces,
   no blank lines, case sensitive):

        BACKUP COMPLETE
        ARCHIVE:/home/user/archives/projects_backup_20240630.tar.gz
        MANIFEST:/home/user/archives/projects_manifest_20240630.log


MANIFEST FILE FORMAT
• One file per line.  
• Field 1: archive-relative path (no leading “./”).  
• Field 2: literal pipe character “|”.  
• Field 3: file size in **bytes** (base-10).  
• Nothing else — no trailing spaces or TABs.

Example (the exact lines that must appear, already sorted):

site1/config.yml|15
site1/index.html|19
site2/README.md|8
site2/data/info.txt|5

(Those four files are the only regular files that exist in the supplied
sample tree, so your manifest should be identical to the example.)

CONSTRAINTS & HINTS
• You have normal user privileges only; no sudo.  
• Do not hard-code paths into the manifest; generate them programmatically
  so future directories could be handled the same way.  
• Feel free to use parallel shells, pipes, subshells or “&&” chains —
  whatever gives you the cleanest solution.  
• The grader will extract the tarball and compare both its contents and
  the manifest, so double-check before announcing completion.
truth:
Initial file-system state before the agent starts:

Directory tree /home/user/projects (permissions 0755 for every directory):
└─ site1/
   ├─ index.html      — contents: "<html>Site1</html>\n"
   └─ config.yml      — contents: "theme: minimal\n"
└─ site2/
   ├─ README.md       — contents: "# Site2\n"
   └─ data/
        └─ info.txt   — contents: "Info\n"

No “/home/user/archives” directory exists yet.

Expected artefacts after a correct run:

1. /home/user/archives (0755).

2. /home/user/archives/projects_manifest_20240630.log  
   Exact byte sequence (including terminating LF at the end):

site1/config.yml|15\n
site1/index.html|19\n
site2/README.md|8\n
site2/data/info.txt|5\n

3. /home/user/archives/projects_backup_20240630.tar.gz  
   •  Contains exactly the following entries, all with prefix “projects/”:
        projects/site1/config.yml
        projects/site1/index.html
        projects/site2/README.md
        projects/site2/data/info.txt
   •  Each entry’s payload is byte-for-byte identical to the original
      files listed above.  
   •  No manifest file or extraneous path is present inside the archive.

4. The agent must output to stdout (captured by the harness) the three
   lines:

BACKUP COMPLETE\n
ARCHIVE:/home/user/archives/projects_backup_20240630.tar.gz\n
MANIFEST:/home/user/archives/projects_manifest_20240630.log\n

Permissions of both new files: 0644.

The automated test will:
•  Verify the manifest file’s existence, permissions and exact contents.  
•  Extract the tarball into a temporary directory and compare the file
   list and payloads with the original source tree.  
•  Ensure that the manifest is not included in the tar archive.  
•  Confirm that the required stdout lines were produced in the correct
   order with no extra text.
